title: Thyroid Nodule - Total Thyroidectomy
chief_complaint: 40-year-old female with 3cm right thyroid nodule found on routine examination. No compressive symptoms. No voice changes. Fine needle aspiration shows papillary thyroid carcinoma (Bethesda VI).

physical_exam.neck: 3cm firm nodule right thyroid lobe, mobile with swallowing, no cervical lymphadenopathy, trachea midline

hidden_data.us_thyroid: 3cm solid hypoechoic nodule with microcalcifications right lobe, no suspicious lymph nodes

correct_diagnosis: Papillary Thyroid Carcinoma (3cm) - Total Thyroidectomy Indicated
key_learning_points: FNA = gold standard for diagnosis (Bethesda system)
Total thyroidectomy for tumors >2cm or bilateral
Central neck dissection if lymph nodes involved

patient_responses.lump: I felt a lump in my neck, no pain

feedback.incorrect_message: This is papillary thyroid carcinoma. The FNA Bethesda VI confirms it.
feedback.attending_note: PTC: Total thyroidectomy + central neck dissection if lymph nodes involved. Post-op RAI if high risk (tumor >4cm, ETE, LN mets). Lifelong levothyroxine. Monitor thyroglobulin and anti-Tg antibodies. Excellent prognosis (>98% 5-year survival).
